title: Open Source Semantic Web Ontology (OSSWO)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6td5d","text":"Open Source Semantic Web Ontology (OSSWO) is an interontological alignment methodology based on the world wide web (WWW) internet infrastructure open standards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"50gbb","text":"Follow each step under the local and international rule of law (RoL).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

(OER).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":64,"length":72},{"key":1,"offset":136,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":144,"length":14},{"key":3,"offset":159,"length":22},{"key":4,"offset":181,"length":12},{"key":5,"offset":198,"length":20},{"key":6,"offset":231,"length":36},{"key":7,"offset":268,"length":41},{"key":8,"offset":316,"length":82},{"key":9,"offset":409,"length":32}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBAF7DE84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBB09AE84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBAFABE84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://ethicalsource.dev"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBAFECE84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://firstdonoharm.dev"}},"4":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBB0C7E84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://firstdonoharm.dev"}},"5":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBB022E84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://contributor-covenant.org"}},"6":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBB0FBE84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://commission.europa.eu/about/departments-and-executive-agencies/digital-services/open-source-strategy-history/european-union-public-licence_en"}},"7":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBB067E84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://interoperable-europe.ec.europa.eu/collection/eupl/how-use-eupl"}},"8":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBAF0FE84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://unesdoc.unesco.org/ark:/48223/pf0000383205?posInSet=11\u0026queryId=c113a623-2bd5-45ce-a4aa-ea2389aa5e95"}},"9":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"34FBAF4EE84011F0AD680A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.unesco.org/en/open-educational-resources"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2hbah","text":"A legalized community in need of a semantic web ontology.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Define open ethics principles to be the rule of law (RoL) regulatory compliance standards of the ontology. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Virtue_ethics https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rule_of_law https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regulatory_compliance Open Ethics Compliance (OEC) by Felipe Ribeiro
Decide the open license and ethical license of the ontology. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_license https://ethicalsource.dev Nonviolent Nonprofit Open Education License (NNOEL) by Felipe Ribeiro

Structure open science methodological dependencies requirements of the ontology engineering procedure. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_science https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scientific_method https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dependency https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ontology_engineering Rule of Law Open Science (RoLOS) by Felipe Ribeiro

Establish your open internet and open source culture requests. My two ontologies on the theme may be of assistance: https://www.academia.edu/143169562/Open_Internet https://www.academia.edu/130285565/Open_Source_Culture

Understand the internet world wide web (WWW) governance structure, including at least: • Internet Governance Forum (IGF) . • Internet Architecture Board (IAB) . • Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) Internet Engineering Steering Group (IESG) . • Internet Research Task Force (IRTF) . • World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) . • International Internet Preservation Consortium (IIPC) . • Internet Society (ISoc) . • World Wide Web (WWW) .
Read the 'Open Source Semantic Web Ontology (OSSWO)' whitepaper. https://www.academia.edu/145734647/Open_Source_Semantic_Web_Ontology_OSSWO_

Research the open education and open pedagogy requirements for the ontology. https://www.academia.edu/130285533/Rule_of_Law_Open_Education_RoLOE_ https://www.academia.edu/130285459/Wikipedagogy Wikipedagogy by Felipe Ribeiro

Learn how to format your open data under the world wide web (WWW) standards: • Open standards . • Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) . • Rule Interchange Format (RIF) . • Uniform Resources Identification (URI) . • Extensible Markup Language (XML) . • Linked Open Data (LOD) . • Linked Open Vocabularies (LOV) . • Resources escription Framework (RDF) . • RDF N-Triples (NT) . • RDF Schema (RDFS) . • XML Schema Definition (XSD) . • Triples Serialization in XML (TriX) .

Understand how to propose an open standard working draft to the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C): • Internet Standard (STD) Request for Comment (RfC) .• Best Current Practice . • Internet Experiment Note . • World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) work group creation . • World Wide Web Consotrium (W3C) web standards publication process .

Learn on how to startup the ontology engineering task force: • Ontology-based reasoning . • Ontology engineering . • Ontology components . • Reliability engineering . • Secure operational development (DevSecOps) .
Develop the ontological controlled vocabulary : • Unstructured data data cleansing . • Texts corpora corpus linguistics . • Controlled vocabulary .

Research ontological databases for ontologies related to your field of development. • Ontology-Driven Data Documentation for Industry Commons (OntoCommons) . • Open Biological and Biomedical Ontology (OBO) . • Research Data Alliance (RDA) . • Open Data Initiative (ODI) .

Define your semantic web approach through:• Web engineering . • Web resource . • Web science . • Semantic web . • Semantic heterogeneity . • Semantic computing . • Semantic technology . • Semantic decision table . • Semantic integration . • Semantic matching . • Semantic unification . • Semantic interoperability .

Comprehend ontology alignment .• Interontological alignment . • Ontology-based reasoning . • Ontology components . • Ontology engineering . • Upper ontologies . • Domain ontologies . • Interontologies .

Set a library information science (LIS) rating system :• Library information science (LIS) . • Library classification . • Diagram flow chart . • Data model . • Rating system digital badges credentialing certification hierarchization .
creator: Felipe Ribeiro
